title: Explainer: median survival, survival-at-t & at-risk numbers
context: Clinicians reading oncology trial results frequently encounter median survival as the headline number, but it is often misread — in particular, when median survival is not reached in one arm, when small at-risk numbers make the tail estimate unreliable, and when survival-at-t (the estimated probability of surviving to a specific timepoint) is conflated with median survival. This explainer builds directly on the KM and censoring explainer (km-006) and is part of the M1 core survival-analysis set. It depends on the authoring template (template-002) and the reproducibility harness (repro-005).
objective: Author, biostatistician-sign-off, and publish the median survival and survival-at-t explainer, covering the definition of median survival (including the 'not reached' scenario), the survival-at-t estimator, the at-risk table's role in assessing reliability, and common misreadings, with a reproducible worked example on open/synthetic data.
acceptanceCriteria: The explainer provides plain and precise definitions of median survival (the time at which estimated survival probability crosses 50%), survival-at-t, and the 'median not reached' scenario.

An explicit commonTrap section covers: treating median survival as a fixed, precise number when few patients remain at risk in the tail; confusing median survival with mean survival; misinterpreting 'not reached' as uninformative when it may indicate high survival.
The at-risk table's role in evaluating the reliability of median survival estimates is explained and included in the howToReadInAPaper checklist.
A worked example on open/synthetic data has a data manifest entry; all figures and numbers are emitted by a committed script and diff-matched by repro-005.
Every conceptual claim is cited to an authoritative methods source; figures have alt-text and are colorblind-safe.
A structural 'educational, not clinical advice' framing field is present.
Biostatistician statsSignoff is recorded; clinical-framing and accessibility reviews passed.
Zero stated statistical falsehoods; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md — Scope (median survival and at-risk tables listed as in-scope)
output: A published, biostatistician-signed-off median survival and survival-at-t explainer with 100% reproducible worked example, full provenance, all claims cited, accessibility passed, and structural 'not clinical advice' frame.
